1.notes: Par 4 opener. One of narrowest fairways on course but ample room between sand area on right and dune grass on left. Good drive leaves short iron to gently undulating green tucked into natural dune area. Pete Dye: 'Furthest from ocean, hit driver freely.'

2.notes: Par 5 with two marsh crossings. Decide how much to bite off on tee shot. Elevated green set between two sand ridges, reachable in two depending on wind. Pete Dye: 'Nothing but sand around the green.'

3.notes: Par 4. Fire across marsh from island tee to extremely wide fairway. Best drives find plateau on left for best look at elevated tabletop green. Half wedge can be treacherous. Miss short or right for difficult up-and-down.

4.notes: Most difficult hole on outward nine. Par 4. Second shot to large green can require nearly every club depending on wind. Bailout area front left. #1 handicap hole. Pete Dye: 'Strictly a driving hole.'

5.notes: Par 3. Hourglass green running away diagonally from right. Large natural sand area from tee to green with steep face cutting into middle. Largest green on course. Must hit appropriate portion or face difficult two-putt.

hazards.notes: Runs from tee to green, steep face cuts into middle

6.notes: Par 4. Perfect drive at center oak with slight draw, avoiding sand area and pond to left. Green open in front, protected left and right by sand. Narrow, deep green with undulation.

7.notes: Par 5. Wind determines strategy for tee shot - decide whether to carry natural dune area intruding from right or play left. Slightly elevated green open in front. Good birdie chance downwind.

hazards.notes: Runs down left side and around back of green

8.notes: Par 3. Seemingly simple but becomes more difficult as pin is cut further into elevated green. Framed by live oaks front left. Narrow as it extends from tee. Miss long or right finds sand. Back third of green 3ft lower.

hazards.notes: Behind green, shouldn't come into play

9.notes: Par 4. Wide fairway sloping down from right makes driving length more important than direction. Green offset - ball must be turning right to left or carry to green. Grassy swale and deep sand areas left and right.

10.notes: Par 4. Elevated tee atop sand dune with panorama of back nine. Drive left-center to crest sets up second shot to green set down into dunes. Large sand area front left, steep faced sand area to back.

11.notes: Par 5. Key is accurate lay-up. Avoid sand areas right of fairway off tee. Second shot: lay back on right side, taking sand area left out of play. Elevated green atop dune ridge.

12.notes: Par 4. Widest fairway on course but gives way to one of narrowest approaches. Good drive sets up downhill second. Approach appears narrow but open in front with rolling collar area left.

13.notes: Par 4. May be most difficult hole on inward nine. Canal runs down entire right side - comes into play on tee shots. Two fairway pot bunkers in play. Green long and narrow with water right, bunkers left.

14.notes: Par 3. Perhaps most beautiful hole but also one of most treacherous. Severely exposed and elevated green. Most exposed putting surface on entire course. Miss front right only safe spot. Dynamic ocean views from tee.

15.notes: Par 4. Seemingly straightforward but often overlooked. Tee shot must find fairway for mid-iron to green set in natural dune area. Difficult to determine wind speed and direction in sheltered green.

16.notes: Par 5. Downwind provides great chance for birdie or eagle - many go for green in two. Extremely deep and dangerous sand area guards left side of green. Advantage to play down right side.

17.notes: Par 3. Pete Dye's favorite hole. All carry over water. Target appears narrow, fiercely guarded by water short and right, two deep sand areas left. Almost a double green with front and back landing areas.

18.notes: Par 4. Best drives hug right side of fairway. Wind determines whether players fire with mid irons or long irons/fairway metals. Dunes on right more severe than any other hole. Pete Dye considers it best par 4 on course.

1.notes: Par 4. Opening hole at Torrey Pines North. 421 yards from blacks. Coastal course with ocean views. Wide landing area off tee.

2.notes: Par 4. 495 yards from blacks. #1 handicap hole. Long dogleg left requiring precise tee shot. Second shot uphill to elevated green.

3.notes: Par 3. 241 yards from blacks. Long par 3 playing downhill with ocean views. Green protected by bunkers front and back.

4.notes: Par 4. 479 yards from blacks. #3 handicap. Sharp dogleg right. Drive must avoid bunkers on the corner. Approach plays longer due to uphill.

5.notes: Par 5. 525 yards from blacks. Reachable for long hitters. Slight dogleg left with bunkers guarding landing area. Green has multiple tiers.

6.notes: Par 4. 416 yards from blacks. #7 handicap. Straightforward par 4 with bunkers framing the green. Ocean views to the right.

7.notes: Par 4. 322 yards from blacks. Short driveable par 4. Risk/reward - can attack green but bunkers and canyon guard aggressively.

8.notes: Par 3. 214 yards from blacks. Long uphill par 3. Plays longer than yardage due to elevation. Bunkers protect front of green.

9.notes: Par 5. 556 yards from blacks. Sweeping dogleg left. Pacific views on approach. Multi-tiered green requires precise distance control.

10.notes: Par 5. 536 yards from blacks. Opens back nine with reachable par 5. Slight dogleg right with water on right side near green.

11.notes: Par 4. 339 yards from blacks. #18 handicap - easiest hole on course. Short par 4 with generous fairway. Good birdie opportunity.

12.notes: Par 3. 203 yards from blacks. #14 handicap. Uphill par 3 with bunkers surrounding green. Wind can be factor.

13.notes: Par 4. 459 yards from blacks. #4 handicap. Demanding dogleg right with canyon on right. Must position tee shot carefully.

14.notes: Par 4. 451 yards from blacks. #6 handicap. Long par 4 with bunkers guarding both sides of fairway and green.

15.notes: Par 3. 202 yards from blacks. #16 handicap. Downhill par 3 with stunning ocean backdrop. Green slopes back to front.

16.notes: Par 4. 393 yards from blacks. #8 handicap. Slight dogleg left with approach uphill. Bunkers guard narrow green.

17.notes: Par 5. 520 yards from blacks. #10 handicap. Sweeping dogleg left with excellent birdie opportunity for aggressive play. Ocean views.

18.notes: Par 4. 486 yards from blacks. #2 handicap. Demanding finishing hole. Long uphill par 4 with bunkers guarding elevated green.

1.notes: Par 4. 426 yards. Opening hole at TPC Twin Cities. Arnold Palmer design. Wide fairway with wetlands on right. Beach bunker style hazards typical of Palmer design.

2.notes: Par 4. 388 yards. Slight dogleg left with water hazard on left side. Bentgrass fairway. Rolling terrain typical of former sod farm site.

3.notes: Par 5. 546 yards. Reachable par 5 with water guarding green. Dogleg left with bunkers on corner. Risk/reward second shot over water.

4.notes: Par 3. 177 yards. Short par 3 over water. Green surrounded by bunkers. Average green size 6,500 sq ft.

5.notes: Par 4. 418 yards. Medium length par 4. Native prairie grasses line both sides. Bentgrass fairway on rolling terrain.

6.notes: Par 5. 571 yards. Long par 5 with water all down right side. Must lay up or carry water to go for green in two.

7.notes: Par 4. 318 yards. Short driveable par 4 featuring Palmer's signature beach bunker. Risk/reward off tee. Aggressive play can pay off.

8.notes: Par 3. 204 yards. Long par 3 with water front and right. Green elevated slightly. One of 72 bunkers on course.

9.notes: Par 4. 475 yards. Long finishing hole for front nine. Water on left threatens aggressive tee shots. Demanding approach.

10.notes: Par 4. 379 yards. Shorter par 4 opening back nine. Water hazard on left. Good birdie opportunity if positioned well off tee.

11.notes: Par 4. 414 yards. Mid-length par 4. Water on right comes into play on approach. Mature oak trees frame the hole.

12.notes: Par 5. 593 yards. Longest hole on course. Dogleg right with water on right throughout. Three-shot hole for most players.

13.notes: Par 3. 228 yards. Longest par 3 on course. Water short and right. Green is large and multi-leveled.

14.notes: Par 4. 423 yards. Slight dogleg left. Water protects left side. Position tee shot right-center for best angle into green.

15.notes: Par 4. 451 yards. Long demanding par 4. Water hazard right of green. Spruce and pine trees line fairway.

16.notes: Par 4. 387 yards. Slight dogleg right. Water on right throughout. Risk/reward tee shot - cut corner over water or play safe left.

17.notes: Par 3. 184 yards. Island-style green surrounded by water. Picturesque but demanding. Wind can be significant factor.

hazards.notes: Water surrounds green on all sides

18.notes: Par 5. 582 yards. Demanding finishing hole. Dogleg left with water down entire left side. Risk/reward second shot. Signature finish.
